content.text: Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) 2.0 is an XML-based federated identity standard for exchanging authentication and authorization data between an Identity Provider (IdP) and a Service Provider (SP). Entra ID acts as the IdP; the application (SaaS tool, web app) is the SP. After the user authenticates at Entra, the IdP sends a signed XML Assertion to the SP proving the user's identity and attributes.

SAML predates OAuth 2.0 and OIDC. It remains mandatory for many enterprise SaaS applications (Salesforce, ServiceNow, Workday, and hundreds of others) that implemented SSO before OIDC existed. When integrating with these applications, SAML is the only option — Entra's Enterprise Applications gallery provides pre-configured SAML templates for thousands of them.

attrs.alt: SAML 2.0 SP-initiated SSO flow showing the 10-step sequence between user/browser, service provider, and Entra ID as identity provider

content.text: The SAML Assertion is a signed XML document that carries identity claims from the IdP to the SP. It is embedded inside a SAMLResponse, which is Base64-encoded and POST-ed by the browser to the SP's Assertion Consumer Service (ACS) URL. The SP must validate the assertion's signature, audience, time window, and InResponseTo value before creating a session.

<!-- Example SAML 2.0 Assertion (abbreviated) issued by Entra ID -->
<saml:Assertion xmlns:saml="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:assertion"
    ID="_assertion-id-12345"
    IssueInstant="2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
    Version="2.0">

  <!-- Issuer: Entra ID's entity ID for your tenant -->
  <saml:Issuer>https://sts.windows.net/{tenant-id}/</saml:Issuer>

  <!-- Signature: RSA-SHA256, signed with Entra's certificate -->
  <ds:Signature xmlns:ds="http://www.w3.org/2000/09/xmldsig#">
    <!-- ... SignedInfo, SignatureValue, X509Certificate ... -->
  </ds:Signature>

  <!-- Subject: the authenticated user -->
  <saml:Subject>
    <saml:NameID Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress">
      alice@contoso.com
    </saml:NameID>
    <saml:SubjectConfirmation Method="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:cm:bearer">
      <saml:SubjectConfirmationData
          InResponseTo="_authnrequest-id-abc"
          NotOnOrAfter="2025-01-15T10:35:00Z"    <!-- 5-min window -->
          Recipient="https://app.example.com/saml/acs" />
    </saml:SubjectConfirmation>
  </saml:Subject>

  <!-- Conditions: audience restriction -->
  <saml:Conditions
      NotBefore="2025-01-15T10:25:00Z"
      NotOnOrAfter="2025-01-15T11:30:00Z">
    <saml:AudienceRestriction>
      <saml:Audience>https://app.example.com</saml:Audience>
    </saml:AudienceRestriction>
  </saml:Conditions>

  <!-- Attribute Statement: user attributes sent to the SP -->
  <saml:AttributeStatement>
    <saml:Attribute Name="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2005/05/identity/claims/name">
      <saml:AttributeValue>Alice Smith</saml:AttributeValue>
    </saml:Attribute>
    <saml:Attribute Name="http://schemas.microsoft.com/identity/claims/objectidentifier">
      <saml:AttributeValue>00000000-0000-0000-66f3-3332eca7ea81</saml:AttributeValue>
    </saml:Attribute>
    <saml:Attribute Name="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ws/2008/06/identity/claims/groups">
      <saml:AttributeValue>Sales-Team-OID</saml:AttributeValue>
      <saml:AttributeValue>All-Employees-OID</saml:AttributeValue>
    </saml:Attribute>
  </saml:AttributeStatement>
</saml:Assertion>
Must match your trusted IdP entity ID
Use to look up or provision the user
Reject if current time exceeds this
Ties assertion to a specific AuthnRequest
Match against stored request ID to prevent replay
Must match your SP entity ID exactly
RSA-SHA256 signed by IdP private key
Verify against IdP's published X.509 certificate

Always validate at the assertion level (not just the outer Response). Some SP libraries accept an unsigned assertion inside a signed Response — this is not secure since the assertion content can be swapped without breaking the outer signature.

Enterprise applications in Entra ID support SAML SSO through the Enterprise Applications blade. For most common SaaS apps, Entra provides pre-built templates that pre-populate the IdP metadata, signing certificate, and attribute mappings. For custom apps, you configure the SP's entity ID, ACS URL, and attribute claims manually.

# Configure a SAML enterprise app in Entra ID via Microsoft Graph
# Requires: Application.ReadWrite.All, AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All

Connect-MgGraph -Scopes "Application.ReadWrite.All"

# Step 1: Get the service principal for the enterprise app
$sp = Get-MgServicePrincipal -Filter "displayName eq 'MyApp-SAML'"

# Step 2: Set the SAML SSO configuration on the SP
$samlParams = @{
    preferredSingleSignOnMode = "saml"
    loginUrl                   = "https://myapp.example.com/login"
    logoutUrl                  = "https://myapp.example.com/logout"
}
Update-MgServicePrincipal -ServicePrincipalId $sp.Id -BodyParameter $samlParams

# Step 3: Configure the Reply URL (ACS URL) and Identifier (Entity ID)
$appId = $sp.AppId
$app = Get-MgApplication -Filter "appId eq '$appId'"

Update-MgApplication -ApplicationId $app.Id -Web @{
    redirectUris = @("https://myapp.example.com/saml/acs")
} -IdentifierUris @("https://myapp.example.com")

# Step 4: Add a claim mapping (attribute statement)
# Map Entra's 'givenname' to the SAML attribute 'FirstName'
New-MgServicePrincipalClaimsMappingPolicy -ServicePrincipalId $sp.Id

# Assign users/groups to the app
$groupId = "<group-object-id>"
New-MgGroupAppRoleAssignment -GroupId $groupId \
    -ResourceId $sp.Id \
    -AppRoleId "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"  # default app role

# Step 5: Download the Entra SAML signing certificate (for the SP to trust)
$cert = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/$($sp.Id)/tokenSigningCertificates"
# Provide the cert's thumbprint and Base64 value to the SP's trusted cert configuration
Write-Host "Certificate thumbprint: $($cert.value[0].thumbprint)"
Entra's SAML SSO endpoints: Sign-on URL = https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/saml2, Sign-out URL = https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/saml2
The Federation Metadata XML at https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/federationmetadata/2007-06/federationmetadata.xml gives SP all IdP details including the signing certificate
Entra's SAML signing certificate has a 3-year lifetime — set a calendar reminder before it expires or the SP will reject all assertions
Assign users or groups to the enterprise app before they can sign in — unassigned users see an AADSTS50105 error
Test with Entra's built-in 'Test SAML SSO' button in the app configuration — it validates the full flow without leaving the admin center
Validating SAML Assertions in Your Application

When your app acts as an SP (accepting SAML from Entra), it must process the SAMLResponse POST-ed to the ACS endpoint. Use a well-tested SAML library rather than parsing XML manually — XML signature validation has subtle attack surfaces (XML signature wrapping, XPath injection) that are easy to get wrong.
Always set strict=True in python3-saml — strict mode enforces NotOnOrAfter, audience, and InResponseTo validation
The ACS endpoint must be HTTPS — the browser POST carries the assertion in plaintext and must not traverse non-TLS links
Store the AuthnRequest ID in the server-side session before redirecting and verify InResponseTo on the ACS response

Attribute names in Entra's SAML assertions use the long claim URI format (http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/...) by default — configure custom attribute names in the Entra Token configuration if the SP expects short names
SAML vs OIDC: Choosing the Right Protocol

Both SAML and OIDC solve federated SSO, but they differ in format, complexity, and ecosystem fit. The choice is usually dictated by what the SP (application) supports — you cannot choose OIDC if the SaaS vendor only implemented SAML. For new applications you control, OIDC is the modern default.

# When to choose SAML vs OIDC
# SAML: XML-based, established in enterprise since 2002, mandatory for some legacy SaaS apps
# OIDC: JSON/JWT-based, simpler to implement, preferred for modern apps and APIs

# Entra ID supports both — use SAML only when the SP mandates it (e.g., older SaaS like
# Salesforce classic SSO config, some government portals, legacy on-prem apps).

# SAML Quirks with Entra:
# 1. Entity ID for Entra as IdP: https://sts.windows.net/{tenant-id}/
#    NOT login.microsoftonline.com — this trips up many integrations
# 2. SAML assertions are signed; the entire Response may or may not be signed separately
#    Always require assertion-level signing (not just Response-level)
# 3. Entra's SAML signing certificate rotates every 3 years — set a reminder to update SP config
# 4. SAML uses POST binding for responses (Base64-encoded XML in a form field)
#    The browser carries the assertion — consider large assertion sizes with many group claims
# 5. SAML Single Logout (SLO) is complex — Entra supports it but many SPs don't implement it
#    Falling back to just clearing the SP session (without IdP SLO) is common
Native — access token for API calls

For net-new custom applications on Azure, use OIDC. Reserve SAML for integrations with SaaS vendors that do not support OIDC, or for legacy on-premises applications that were originally integrated with AD FS via SAML.
Customizing SAML Claims and Attribute Statements

The SAML assertion's attribute statement carries user profile data from Entra to the SP. By default, Entra sends a minimal claim set (NameID, immutable ID, UPN). Most SaaS apps require additional attributes like first name, last name, email, and department. Configure these in Entra's Token configuration blade or via a Claims Mapping Policy through Graph API.

The NameID format controls how the user is identified. Email-address format sends the UPN; persistent format sends a stable opaque ID derived from the object ID. Choose based on what the SP uses to match users in its own database — changing it after provisioning causes user account matching failures.

# Configure SAML attribute claims in Entra ID via PowerShell + Graph
# Claims are the attribute statements sent inside the SAML assertion to the SP

Connect-MgGraph -Scopes "Policy.ReadWrite.ApplicationConfiguration", "Application.ReadWrite.All"

# Get the service principal for the SAML app
$sp = Get-MgServicePrincipal -Filter "displayName eq 'MyApp-SAML'"

# Create a Claims Mapping Policy
# This customizes which Entra attributes appear as SAML attribute statements
$claimsMappingPolicy = @{
    definition = @(
        '{"ClaimsMappingPolicy": {'
        + '"Version": 1,'
        + '"IncludeBasicClaimSet": true,'
        + '"ClaimsSchema": ['
        + '{"Source": "user", "ID": "givenname",   "SamlClaimType": "FirstName"},'
        + '{"Source": "user", "ID": "surname",     "SamlClaimType": "LastName"},'
        + '{"Source": "user", "ID": "mail",        "SamlClaimType": "EmailAddress"},'
        + '{"Source": "user", "ID": "department",  "SamlClaimType": "Department"},'
        + '{"Source": "user", "ID": "objectid",    "SamlClaimType": "UserID"}'
        + ']}}'
    )
    displayName = "MyApp SAML Claims"
    isOrganizationDefault = $false
}

$policy = New-MgPolicyClaimMappingPolicy \
    -DisplayName $claimsMappingPolicy.displayName \
    -Definition $claimsMappingPolicy.definition

# Assign the policy to the service principal
New-MgServicePrincipalClaimMappingPolicyByRef \
    -ServicePrincipalId $sp.Id \
    -OdataId "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/policies/claimsMappingPolicies/$($policy.Id)"

# Common NameID formats Entra supports:
# urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress  -> user's UPN
# urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:persistent   -> stable opaque ID (oid)
# urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:transient    -> session-scoped ephemeral ID
# urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecified  -> Entra chooses (usually UPN)

SAML Single Logout (SLO) terminates the user's session at the IdP and all connected SPs simultaneously. SP-initiated SLO starts at the SP: the user clicks logout, the SP sends a LogoutRequest to Entra (redirect binding), Entra signs the user out and sends a LogoutResponse back. Entra ID also supports IdP-initiated SLO, where signing out from the Entra My Apps portal terminates sessions at all enrolled SPs.

In practice, many SPs do not implement the SLO endpoint correctly or at all, so coordinated logout is often incomplete. If your app handles sensitive data, expire server-side sessions aggressively (short session lifetime) rather than relying on SLO alone.
Store the `saml_session_index` from the ACS assertion in the server session — required for the LogoutRequest to Entra
The SP's SLO endpoint must be HTTPS and must be registered in Entra's app configuration
If the SP does not support SLO, the browser session persists even after IdP logout — direct users to the Entra My Apps portal for a full sign-out
Entra supports both HTTP-Redirect and HTTP-POST bindings for SLO; Redirect binding (signed in the URL) is simpler to configure

SAML failures often produce cryptic error codes. The most useful debugging tool is inspecting the raw SAMLResponse XML — decode the Base64 value from the ACS POST body to see exactly what Entra sent. The SAML-tracer browser extension does this automatically, intercepting all SAML documents in the browser's network traffic.

# SAML Troubleshooting: decode and inspect SAMLResponse from browser
# The SAMLResponse field in the ACS POST is Base64-encoded XML

import base64, sys
from xml.dom import minidom

def decode_saml_response(b64_response: str) -> str:
    """Pretty-print the SAMLResponse XML for debugging."""
    xml_bytes = base64.b64decode(b64_response)
    return minidom.parseString(xml_bytes).toprettyxml(indent='  ')

# In browser DevTools → Network → find the ACS POST → Form Data → copy SAMLResponse
# Then paste into the SAML decode script:
# python3 -c "import base64; print(base64.b64decode('<paste here>').decode())"

# Common SAML errors and causes:

# AADSTS50011: 'The reply URL does not match'
# -> The ACS URL in the Entra config doesn't exactly match what the SP sends
#    Check for trailing slashes, port numbers, http vs https

# AADSTS75005: 'The application does not support the SAML request'
# -> The AuthnRequest format or NameID format is not supported
#    Try removing the RequestedAuthnContext or NameIDPolicy from the request

# AADSTS750054: 'SAMLRequest or SAMLResponse must be present'
# -> The IdP-initiated URL is wrong — use the app's Login URL in Entra, not the SAML endpoint

# 'Audience is invalid' in SP (assertion validation error):
# -> SP entity ID in Entra doesn't match what the SP expects in the Audience element
#    Must be identical strings, including trailing slashes

# 'NotOnOrAfter expired':
# -> Clock skew between IdP and SP > 5 minutes
#    Use NTP on both; most libraries allow a configurable clock skew tolerance

# Debug with SAML DevTools browser extension:
# Intercepts all SAMLRequest/SAMLResponse and shows decoded XML
# Available for Chrome ('SAML-tracer') and Firefox ('SAML-tracer')
Use the SAML Tracer extension (Chrome/Firefox) — it decodes SAMLRequest and SAMLResponse automatically as you step through the flow
The Entra sign-in logs show SAML-specific error details: search for the CorrelationId from the error page in Entra Admin Center → Sign-in logs
Certificate mismatch: when Entra's signing cert is renewed, download the new certificate from Entra and update it in the SP's trusted certificate configuration
Test IdP-initiated SSO from the Entra My Apps portal (myapps.microsoft.com) — useful when SP-initiated fails due to incorrect redirect configuration

SAML 2.0 is the XML-based federation protocol used by thousands of enterprise SaaS applications for SSO. Entra ID acts as the SAML Identity Provider, issuing signed assertions after the user authenticates. The SP validates the assertion's signature, audience, and time constraints before granting access.
SAML assertions are signed XML documents carrying user identity and attribute claims from Entra to the SP
Configure SAML apps in Entra via Enterprise Applications — use pre-built gallery templates for common SaaS
Always validate assertion-level signatures, NotOnOrAfter, Audience, and InResponseTo — never skip any
Use a tested SAML library (python3-saml, passport-saml, OneLogin) with strict mode enabled
Choose OIDC for new apps you control; use SAML only when the SP mandates it
